compiled_state.title: GREEN: Implement check_usage.py with OAuth polling, flock caching, and 3-tier verdicts
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Implement check_usage.py — the core Python module for usage-aware throttle verdicts. Makes all RED tests from Task 1 pass.

### Files to create
- plugins/dso/scripts/check_usage.py

### Implementation details

**Functions:**
- get_oauth_token() — retrieve OAuth token from macOS Keychain (security CLI) or Linux (~/.claude/.credentials.json → claudeAiOauth.accessToken). Return None if not found.
- poll_usage(token) — GET https://api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage with Bearer token, anthropic-beta header, --max-time 8. Return dict with five_hour and seven_day utilization. Handle 429 (return None to signal rate limit).
- read_cache(cache_path, ttl_seconds) — read JSON cache file, return None if missing/stale/corrupt. TTL floor: max(ttl_seconds, 60).
- write_cache(cache_path, lock_path, data) — write cache atomically (temp + rename) with fcntl.flock on separate lock_path. Fields: timestamp, five_hour_pct, seven_day_pct, resets_at.
- compute_verdict(five_hour_pct, seven_day_pct) — apply threshold logic: ≥95%/≥98% → 2 (paused), ≥90%/≥95% → 1 (throttled), else → 0 (unlimited).
- main() — orchestrate: check credentials → CI fallback → read cache → poll if stale → write cache → compute verdict → exit with code.

**Exit codes:** 0=unlimited, 1=throttled, 2=paused
**Cache path:** ~/.cache/claude/usage-cache.json (mkdir -p before write)
**Lock path:** ~/.cache/claude/usage-cache.lock (separate from cache file)
**CI fallback:** if no credentials, print USAGE_SOURCE: no-credentials, exit 0
**429 handling:** with existing cache: serve last verdict but degrade unlimited→throttled (fail-closed); without cache: exit 1
**Security:** never log/print the OAuth token value; only log verdict and utilization percentages

### Document cache contract
Include docstring or code comments documenting the cache JSON format:
```json
{
  "timestamp": 1234567890,
  "five_hour_pct": 0.85,
  "seven_day_pct": 0.72,
  "resets_at": "2026-04-06T18:00:00Z"
}
```
TTL semantics: stale cache = serve last verdict + attempt background refresh.

## File Impact
- plugins/dso/scripts/check_usage.py (create)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] plugins/dso/scripts/check_usage.py exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/check_usage.py
- [ ] All RED tests from Task 1 now pass (GREEN)
  Verify: python3 -m pytest $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test_check_usage.py --tb=short -q
- [ ] Exit code contract: exit 0 when below thresholds
  Verify: python3 -c "from plugins.dso.scripts.check_usage import compute_verdict; assert compute_verdict(0.5, 0.5) == 0"
- [ ] Exit code contract: exit 1 at 90% 5hr
  Verify: python3 -c "from plugins.dso.scripts.check_usage import compute_verdict; assert compute_verdict(0.90, 0.5) == 1"
- [ ] Exit code contract: exit 2 at 95% 5hr
  Verify: python3 -c "from plugins.dso.scripts.check_usage import compute_verdict; assert compute_verdict(0.95, 0.5) == 2"
- [ ] Cache contract documented in code
  Verify: grep -q "five_hour_pct" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/check_usage.py
- [ ] OAuth token never logged
  Verify: ! grep -n "print.*token\|log.*token\|echo.*token" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/check_usage.py | grep -v "# security"
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/run-all.sh

comments.body: IMPORTANT: All tests MUST mock HTTP calls. Never hit the real Anthropic OAuth endpoint. Use unittest.mock.patch for urllib.request.urlopen. The check_usage.py module should be structured so HTTP calls are mockable (e.g., a dedicated poll_usage() function that can be patched).
comments.author: Test

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 6/6: Done ✓ — All acceptance criteria verified
comments.author: Test

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 6/6: Done ✓ — check_usage.py implemented, 16/16 tests pass, review score 5/5. Commit 71126bb1.
